data.title: Verve extends gains as Cantor Fitzgerald upgrades on data for cholesterol drug
data.description:
Shares of Verve Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VERV) climbed for the second straight session on Tuesday after Cantor Fitzgerald upgraded the stock based on early-stage trial data the gene editing company posted for its cholesterol therapy VERVE-102.

Verve (NASDAQ:VERV) closed 26% higher on Monday after the readout, which included initial data from its Heart-2 Phase 1b trial for VERVE-102 for two patient groups characterized by high levels of LDL-C, also known as bad cholesterol.

Cantor analyst Rick Bienkowski upgraded VERV to Overweight from Neutral, arguing that Heart-2 data, which represented three dose levels of VERVE-102, exceeded the firm’s expectations for the one-time therapy in terms of both safety and efficacy.

Bienkowski pointed out that the readout didn’t indicate any treatment-related adverse events or any clinically significant changes in liver enzymes ALT, AST, or platelets.

Arguing that there appeared to be no relationship between dose responses and any measure of safety, Bienkowski said that this could be “a positive indicator for safety in the ongoing higher dose cohort (0.7 mg/kg) and the potential switch to fixed doses of VERVE-102 in Phase 2 trials.”

Citing the 53% LDL-C reduction seen in the study’s 0.6 mg/kg dose cohort, the analyst added that VERVE-102 could be "best-in-class for achieving LDL-C goals in heterozygous familial hypercholesterolemia (HeFH),” a genetic condition linked to high cholesterol.

data.title: Ingersoll-Rand acquired G&D Chillers, Advanced Gas for $27M
data.description:
• Ingersoll Rand (NYSE:IR) has acquired G & D Chillers and Advanced Gas Technologies for a combined purchase price of ~$27M to further grow the company’s air treatment portfolio.
  • The execution of bolt-on acquisition strategy continues to enhance the company’s durable financial profile by adding highly complementary products and capabilities focused on high-growth, sustainable end markets.
  • These acquisitions will expand Ingersoll Rand’s product and technology portfolio with additional chiller and onsite gas generation offerings and support the company’s in-region for the region strategy.
  • The acquisitions have a combined pre-synergy Adjusted EBITDA purchase multiple of high-single digits.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow inch higher as traders continue to play 'guess-the-tariffs'
data.description:
Wall Street's major market averages observe a modest move higher on Tuesday after a modest gain to start the week.

Early on and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.2%, the S&P 500 (SP500) was +0.2%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.2%.

"With the latest advance for equities, that now means that the S&P 500 has recovered to -4.67% beneath its level at the tariff announcement, and -12.01% beneath its mid-February peak," Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said. "That’s clearly a significant decline, but it’s still some distance from a normal recession decline, or even some of the larger non-recession bear markets of recent years like 2022, which saw a -25% peak-to-trough fall. So it’s clear that investors aren’t convinced that a recession is inevitable just yet."

Investors were still trying to gauge what kinds of tariff landscape the global economy will face. 

President Donald Trump has hinted at a reprieve for the 25% tariffs on automobile and auto part imports, while the administration has begun investigating the impact of pharmaceutical and semiconductor imports on national security, likely setting the stage for tariffs on the sectors.

Trump "signaled a possible retreat from auto tariffs to allow companies to relocate production to the US," UBS chief economist Paul Donovan said. "There are some challenges to this thinking. Relocation would not be quick. Relocation would add costs (unnecessarily duplicating factories). Relocation would require long-term investment in an erratic policy environment."

Treasury yields were slightly higher after recent declines. The 10-year yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.38%.

On the economic calendar, the Empire State Manufacturing Index rose to -8.10 from -20.00 in March, compared with the -12.80 consensus level, according to a New York Federal Reserve survey released on Tuesday.

At the same time, U.S. import prices slipped 0.1% M/M in March. Export prices, meanwhile, were unchanged over the month

"US March import and export price data does not show the inflation from tariffs (that is applied after the import prices are calculated)," Donovan said. "Monitoring the trends in import price data should confirm that the tariff tax burden is paid by the US, as is generally the case."

data.title: Autodesk holder T. Rowe Price to support Starboard in proxy fight - Reuters
data.description:
T. Rowe Price Investment Management plans to vote for activist Starboard Value's director nominees in the fund's proxy fight with Autodesk (NASDAQ:ADSK), according to a Reuters report on Monday, which cited a person familiar with the matter. 

T. Rowe Price Investment Management and Autodesk declined to comment to Reuters on Monday. Starboard couldn't immediately be reached for comment.

Starboard, which has a $500 million stake in Autodesk, formally nominated three candidates to Autodesk's board late last month, including Starboard CEO Jeff Smith.  The hedge fund is concerned about the stock's underperformance as well as the need for improved margins and profitability.

Autodesk rose 1.4% on Tuesday.

data.title: Société Générale lowers China GDP forecasts after rising US tariffs and economic challenges
data.description:
Société Générale has downgraded China’s 2025 and 2026 GDP growth forecasts to 4%, citing severe disruptions from escalating U.S. tariffs and deflationary pressures.

According to the investment bank, the effective U.S. tariff rate on Chinese exports surged from 13% to around 100% this year, with exemptions limited to select electronics and industrial goods.

“This represents a once-in-a-century trade shock,” said Michelle Lam, Greater China economists at Société Générale, noting the direct export impact could slash 2% from GDP and requires urgent policy intervention.

The tariff spike is projected to reduce China’s exports to the U.S. by 70%, despite exemptions covering 30% of shipments.

“Even with transhipment strategies to bypass tariffs, the collapse in trade volume will ripple through supply chains,” the investment bank analysts said.

Société Générale estimates 27M jobs linked to U.S. exports — 3% of China’s workforce — are at risk, potentially raising unemployment by 2 percentage points and cutting household income by 1% of GDP.

To counter the shock, Chinese policymakers are expected to deploy stimulus worth 2.5% of GDP (RMB 3.5T) over several quarters.

“The scale mirrors past crises, but rebalancing toward domestic consumption is now unavoidable,” Lam said, noting a late April Politburo meeting could greenlight measures.

Société Générale emphasized that social stability hinges on mitigating labor market fallout, with manufacturing investment likely to stagnate as firms accelerate overseas diversification.

Deflationary pressures are expected to intensify, with oil price declines and export oversupply pulling CPI toward 0% and PPI below -3% in 2025.

“Food and industrial goods will face acute deflation, though housing market stabilization might offer minor relief,” Lam concluded.

While a U.S. policy “pause” could ease tariffs, according to the investment bank, uncertainty will linger, with risks of financial decoupling further clouding China’s economic outlook.

data.title: Turing Medical names Kevin King as president and CEO
data.description:
• Turing Medical has announced the appointment of Kevin King, former CEO of iRhythm Technologies (NASDAQ:IRTC), Affymetrix and Thomson Reuters Healthcare, as the Co.’s new President and CEO.
  • Under King's leadership, Turing Medical is aiming to launch a clinical trial of its pioneering Personalized Adaptive Cortical Electro-stimulation Therapy for Treatment Resistant Depression.
  • “Bringing Kevin King on as CEO marks a defining moment for Turing,” said Nico Dosenbach, co-founder of Turing Medical.

data.title: KKR upgraded to Buy at HSBC after stock's slump
data.description:
HSBC upgraded KKR (NYSE:KKR) to Buy as the stock's recent selloff makes its risk-reward equation more favorable.

Year-to-date, KKR's (NYSE:KKR) stock slid 29%, a steeper drop than the S&P 500's 7.5% decline and XLF's 1.2% dip.

"Notwithstanding heightened macro uncertainty, we think the significant correction in share prices offers opportunity," analyst Vikram Gandhi wrote in a note to clients.

With the macro uncertainty, ramifications for initial public offerings and mergers & acquisitions complicate the exit environment for the alternative asset management sector. In addition, that could lead to delayed and/or smaller capital raising for new fund launches, the analyst said.

However, he believes alt-asset manager stocks have dropped more than warranted.

HSBC's Gandhi applies an 18.3x price-earnings multiple to HSBC's 2026 EPS estimate, bringing KKR's (KKR) price target to $119, implying ~17% upside potential and down from its prior PT of $170.

HSBC keeps its Buy rating on Brookfield Asset Management (BAM) and Hold on Blackstone (BX).

The Buy rating on KKR (KKR) contrasts with the SA Quant rating of Hold and aligns with the average SA Analyst rating and average Wall Street rating, both at Buy.

data.title: Bitdeer doubles down on bitcoin mining as tariffs disrupt supply chain for rigs - report
data.description:
Singapore-based Bitdeer Technologies Group (NASDAQ:BTDR) is prioritizing in-house bitcoin (BTC-USD) mining rather than selling rigs to external operators, as U.S. President Donald Trump's tariff policies have interrupted the supply chain for mining rigs, according to a Tuesday media report that cited a company executive.

On top of supply-chain disruptions, the crypto market is cooling as demand for mining rigs loses momentum. The hashprice of bitcoin (BTC-USD), a key metric for mining profitability, has been stuck near all-time lows after last year's halving event slashed miners' rewards.

Taking advantage of Trump's 90-day tariff pause announced April 10, Bitdeer (NASDAQ:BTDR) aims to ship mining rigs from Southeast Asia to the U.S., said Jeff LaBerge, head of capital markets and strategic initiatives at the company, Bloomberg reported.

Meanwhile, with some customers pushing back pre-order deliveries, he added, Bitdeer (BTDR) is diverting those rigs to its operations in Bhutan and Norway (i.e., outside the U.S.).

In addition, the company is planning to start manufacturing machines in the U.S. starting in the back half of 2025, a move "we've been planning for a long time," LaBerge said. “We want to bring jobs and manufacturing back to America.”

“The entire industry is trying to get clarity on how these tariffs might apply,” LaBerge added, as quoted by Bloomberg. “As of now, our understanding is that chips out of Taiwan remain exempt, but we’re modeling for higher costs just in case.”

BTDR shares were off 4.3% in Tuesday morning trading, bringing its year-to-date loss to 65%.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Truth Social-branded SMAs will compliment ETFs offering, says Trump Media
data.description:
Trump Media and Technology Group (NASDAQ:DJT) has tied up with asset manager Yorkville America Equities and Index Technologies Group to launch Truth Social-branded Separately Managed Accounts.

The initial lineup of the SMA strategies will include themes based on "Faith & Values," "Liberty & Security," "Energy Independence," and "Made in America," the companies said Tuesday.

Yorkville and ITG are expected to apply "advanced algorithms" to score and rank companies within each theme, and the SMAs would complement its planned launch of ETFs later this year.

"Each strategy is constructed using a proprietary, data-driven framework that combines financial performance with our proprietary values-based metrics… Portfolios will be rebalanced quarterly to reflect market dynamics and maintain alignment with thematic goals," according to the joint statement.

The company's investment in the ETFs and SMAs are part of its broader financial services and fintech strategy, for which Charles Schwab (NYSE:SCHW) has custodied $250M.

data.title: Global recession from a trade war is seen as top market tail risk, BofA survey finds
data.description:
A potential trade war ignited by the policies of President Donald Trump’s administration—and the threat of a resulting global recession—has emerged as the top market tail risk, according to Bank of America's latest Global Fund Manager Survey. The findings highlight growing concern among global investors about geopolitical tensions and their ripple effects on financial markets.

A striking 80% of fund managers surveyed identified a trade war leading to a global recession as the most significant tail risk facing markets today. This marks the highest level of consensus around a single risk factor since BofA began conducting the survey 15 years ago. 

Beyond the looming threat of a trade war, other concerns were also on fund managers' radar, albeit to a far lesser extent. Inflationary pressures prompting aggressive interest rate hikes by the Federal Reserve ranked as the second-highest tail risk, garnering 10% of responses. 

Meanwhile, 7% of participants cited a potential crash in the U.S. dollar—triggered by a foreign buyers’ strike—as a key threat. Only 1% of respondents pointed to a possible bubble in artificial intelligence as the greatest market risk. 

The sharp focus on trade and recession fears underscores how geopolitical instability is increasingly shaping investor sentiment, especially as markets attempt to navigate a fragile post-pandemic recovery and shifting central bank policies. 

Below is a chart provided by Bank of America highlighting the distribution of tail-risk concerns among fund managers:

Market Tracking ETFs: (NYSEARCA:DIA), (NYSEARCA:DDM), (NYSEARCA:DOG), (NYSEARCA:DXD), (NYSEARCA:SDOW), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:UPRO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS), (NYSEARCA:SPXU), (NASDAQ:QQQ), (NASDAQ:QQQM), (NYSEARCA:QLD), (NASDAQ:TQQQ), (NYSEARCA:QID), and (NASDAQ:SQQQ).
